Description_Effects: Global demand collapse followed by container shortages and port congestion.
~400 ships delayed; billions in trade affected; forced diversion around Africa.
Grain shipment disruption; energy trade realignment; sanctions on Russian exports.
Low water forced transit restrictions; reduced capacity; higher freight costs.
Attacks caused Asia-Europe route disruptions and diversions around Africa.
Geopolitical tensions disrupted shipping and energy flows; oil price spikes.
Brief military exchange and Iranian threats; caused short-term oil price volatility and insurance premiums to rise before de-escalation.
Joint strikes on Iran triggered IRGC attacks on commercial shipping; functional closure of the Strait causing massive global energy shock.
Commitments by China to purchase US agricultural and energy products.
Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism forced European importers to report emissions; trade shifted to low-carbon partners.
Significant tariffs on Chinese EVs, steel, and semiconductors; caused massive front-loading of imports.
Strike by longshoremen suspended until Jan 2025; caused massive diversions to West Coast ports and air freight.
Severed access to a major US East Coast port; diverted bulk coal and automotive cargo for months.
Explosions at Tianjin disrupted hazardous cargo handling, tanker operations, and North China port logistics. Relevant to China trade with EU, USA, Japan, Korea, and major commodity partners.

Hanjin filed for receivership, leaving vessels and containers stranded across major trade lanes. Useful as a concrete network shock for reporters exposed to containerised trade via Asia-Europe and Trans-Pacific routes.

Event_Name: Hurricane Harvey Gulf Port and Refining Disruption
Description_Effects: Hurricane Harvey shut or slowed major Texas ports and knocked out large volumes of refining capacity, disrupting petroleum, chemicals, grain, and container flows tied to the USA and its major trade partners.

The first tranche of reciprocal US-China tariffs took effect, materially slowing and re-routing bilateral trade, especially across Pacific container corridors. Defensible for USA, China, EU spillovers, and agricultural or industrial trade shifts.
Drought-driven low water forced Rhine barges to sail partially loaded, raising freight costs and slowing coal, chemicals, grains, and oil product movements into the European industrial core.

Contaminated Russian crude halted Druzhba flows into parts of Europe and forced refiners to seek alternative supply. Relevant to reporters and partners exposed to European energy trade and substitution effects.

COVID controls at Yantian caused severe export backlogs and vessel queues, disrupting China-Europe and Trans-Pacific container flows. Highly relevant to China, USA, EU reporters and their manufacturing trade partners.
Closure of Meishan terminal after a COVID case intensified congestion and diversions across one of the world’s largest ports, slowing East Asia-Europe and Trans-Pacific cargo movement.
Shanghai COVID lockdowns clogged trucking, warehousing, factory output, and port logistics, slowing goods flows across China-linked global supply chains.

Event_Name: Ukraine Port Suspension and Black Sea Blockade
Description_Effects: Ukraine suspended commercial shipping at its ports after Russia invaded, halting major grain and oilseed exports and hitting Black Sea trade routes relevant to Europe, Turkey, Egypt, and global food importers.
Russia withdrew from the grain corridor, removing safe-passage guarantees for ships from Ukrainian ports and renewing trade friction across Black Sea agricultural routes.
